originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&flags` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= CROSS_COMPILE true) (= WIN32 true) (= NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES false))
model:
(define-fun WIN32 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CROSS_COMPILE () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 42: `flags |= T_ESCAPE_SHELL_CMD` in the #else branch (NEED_ENHANCED=F). flags set in loop but never read — dead store. No '// ERROR' → new bug not in VBDB. CROSS_COMPILE=T prevents NEED_ENHANCED from being defined via WIN32 (else block skipped). NEED_ENHANCED=F is consistent. Dead store occurs whenever NEED_ENHANCED=F. WIN32=T is unnecessary — with CROSS_COMPILE=T, WIN32 cannot define NEED_ENHANCED anyway. PC over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
